Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video uploaded to the application.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the video was uploaded.

storageUrl.description: URL of the video in cloud storage.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL of the video's thumbnail image.

Page.title: Page
Page.description: Represents a static page in the application (e.g., About, Contact).

title.description: Title of the page (e.g., About Us, Contact).

slug.description: URL-friendly slug for the page (e.g., about-us, contact).

definition.description: Stores static page content. Publicly readable.

firestore.reasoning: This Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). It segregates data with different access needs and uses path-based ownership where applicable.  The `videos` collection stores video metadata, and the `pages` collection stores static page content.  Both collections are globally readable. The structure is designed to support simple, robust security rules that avoid `get()` calls. Specifically, the structure uses segregation such that different access patterns are stored in different collections to avoid any security complexity. Since global read is available in both the `videos` and `pages` collections, QAPs are satisfied.
